qa_scores_gemini.notes: Excellent implementation of Asteroids with accurate inertial physics, screen wrapping, asteroid splitting, and a functional UFO enemy.

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable and feature-rich Asteroids clone with solid movement, shooting, waves, lives, pause, and restart, but visual style is only partially faithful and there are logic gaps such as no ship-vs-UFO collision, possible repeated preventDefault side effects, and some unused/misaligned state variables.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Comprehensive Asteroids implementation with ship physics, asteroid splitting, UFO enemies, screen wrapping, invulnerability on respawn, extra life, pause, high score persistence, and a clean vector aesthetic; minor issues include no ship-UFO collision and bullet limit of 4 with no rapid-fire support.

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable with thrust/rotation/shooting, wrapping, splitting asteroids and basic game states, but it has notable logic bugs including no initial asteroids on the title screen, broken high-score persistence/update, UFO wrap/exit behavior issues, and unsafe array splicing during collision updates.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: Excellent implementation of Asteroids with smooth inertial physics, screen wrapping, UFO logic, and a complete game loop.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Core mechanics work well including ship movement, shooting, asteroid splitting, UFO, and screen wrapping, but splice-in-forEach bugs can cause missed collisions or index errors, the idle screen lacks background asteroids since initWave isn't called before playing, high score is never updated, and the ship collision radius is undefined (no ship.r property).
